info.name: e-commerce
info.description:
## e-commerce API

A full-featured e-commerce backend API built with **Node.js** and **Express**, running locally at `http://localhost:3000/api/v1`. This collection covers the complete lifecycle of an e-commerce platform, organized into the following functional modules:

- **Auth** — User registration, login, token refresh, and password management.
- **User** — View and update user profiles, manage account details.
- **Category & SubCategory** — CRUD operations for product categories and their nested sub-categories.
- **Products** — Create, read, update, and soft-delete products, with support for filtering (e.g., by price).
- **Cart** — Add items to cart, view cart contents, update quantities, remove items, and apply coupons.
- **Orders** — Place and manage orders; view all orders or filter by user or ID.
- **HR Management System** — Internal admin module covering:
  - **Staff** — Add, update, retrieve, and remove staff members.
  - **Deductions** — Manage salary deductions per staff member.
  - **Attendance** — Track check-in and check-out records.

> All endpoints target a local development server. Authentication is required for most write operations.

item.name: auth
item.description: Handles all authentication-related operations for the e-commerce platform. Includes endpoints for user registration, login, logout, password reset, and token management. These endpoints are the entry point for securing access to the rest of the API.

item.name: user
item.description: Contains endpoints for managing user accounts. Covers retrieving user profiles, updating personal information, changing passwords, and deleting accounts. These routes are typically protected and require an authenticated session.

item.name: Category
item.description: Manages product categories within the platform. Provides endpoints to create, retrieve, update, and delete top-level categories used to organize and classify products in the store.

item.name: subCategory
item.description: Handles subcategories that fall under main product categories. Supports creating, updating, and deleting subcategories, allowing for a more granular and structured product classification system.

item.name: products
item.description: Covers all product-related operations in the store. Includes endpoints for creating, updating, soft-deleting, and retrieving products, with support for filtering by price and other attributes. Admin-only routes are included for product management.

item.name: cart
item.description: Manages the shopping cart functionality. Includes endpoints to add items to the cart, view the current cart, update item quantities, remove specific items, and clear the entire cart. Designed to support the full cart lifecycle before checkout.

item.name: order

request.description:
Processes the checkout for the authenticated user's cart by placing an order.

**Method:** POST  
**Endpoint:** `/api/v1/orders/checkout`

**Authentication:**  
Requires a Bearer/user JWT token passed in the `Authorization` header. The token must belong to a user with the `user` role.

**Request Body:**
| Field      | Type   | Description                        |
|------------|--------|------------------------------------|
| `quantity` | Number | The quantity of items to order     |

**Notes:**
- The user must be authenticated via a valid JWT token.
- The request creates a new order based on the current cart contents and the specified quantity.

item.description: Handles order processing and management. Provides endpoints to place orders (checkout), view all orders, retrieve a specific order by ID, and manage order status. Supports both user-facing and admin-facing order operations.

item.name: HR management system
item.description: Groups all HR-related endpoints within the e-commerce platform. Covers staff management (add, update, retrieve, soft-delete), deduction tracking (add, update, retrieve, delete), and attendance management (check-in, check-out). This folder serves as the internal HR module for managing employees and their records.
